stat_of_week: 9-0 - Wisconsin Rapids Reapers swept the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs in every single category, the most dominant single-week performance of the season and the first 9-0 shutout on record.
thriller_note: BaseballPro77 squeaked by on the strength of stolen bases and RBI despite a bloated 5.28 ERA, while Benchwarmers' superior pitching wasn't enough to overcome a near-powerless lineup — a one-category swing either way flips this result, and both teams remain dangerously close to the middle of the pack.
key_storyline: Wisconsin Rapids Reapers delivered the most dominant performance of the season, posting a 9-0 shutout of the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs behind Ben Rice's extraordinary week (9 R, 3 HR, 9 RBI, .548 OBP), Mason Miller's filthy 0.30 WHIP out of the bullpen, and a pitching staff that held opponents to a 2.48 ERA with a league-best 0.84 WHIP. The Wonderdogs, meanwhile, were completely shut out in every category — not a single win to show — suffering one of the worst single-week collapses in league history despite having three quality starts from their rotation. Coming off prior power rankings that placed them fifth in the league, the Wonderdogs now face serious questions about roster depth beyond their pitching, while the Reapers announce themselves as a legitimate contender after back-to-back quiet weeks.
lucky_reason: Despite ranking near the bottom in home runs, strikeouts, and overall offensive production, Benchwarmers managed to steal a 5-4 win with a luck delta of +0.58 — the highest in the league this week — thanks to favorable category distribution rather than top-end performance.
unlucky_reason: General Hospital posted a razor-thin 2.10 ERA, a strong .363 OBP, and won six categories on their own merits, yet their luck delta of -0.52 — tied for worst in the league — suggests their overall stat profile was strong enough to have expected a much larger winning margin against the Athletic Park Dragons.
records_broken: Abbotsford-Colby Twins tied the all-time single-week strikeout record with 77 K, matching their own mark set in Week 2 (previous record: 77, Abbotsford-Colby Twins, Week 2). Wisconsin Rapids Reapers set a new record for lowest single-week WHIP by a winning team with 0.84, surpassing the previous benchmark. General Hospital set a new record for lowest ERA by a winning team at 2.10, establishing a new season mark.
power_rankings: 1. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Historic 9-0 shutout anchored by Ben Rice's monster week and Mason Miller's 0.30 WHIP; this team just announced it belongs at the top | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Most consistent team in the league across all four weeks, 8-1 category win this week, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both mashing with Chase Burns dealing — the floor here is elite | same
3. BaseballPro77 | Gutted out a 5-4 win on fumes offensively, but the roster depth and active management keep them near the top; the ERA concern is real and needs fixing fast | down
4. General Hospital | Six category wins with a 2.10 ERA and only 5 HR — this offense has legitimate power gaps but the pitching and on-base skills carry them; Kumar Rocker addition could be huge | up
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP and a staff-wide 2.25 ERA powered a 6-4 win; tied the league K record and the rotation with Ashcraft, Luzardo, and Burke is genuinely deep | up
6. Riverside Roughnecks | Exploded for 38 runs and 10 HR in a 7-3 win — Otto Lopez, Xavier Edwards, Jackson Merrill, Kerry Carpenter all contributing; is this the breakout week they needed? | up
7. Athletic Park Dragons | Lost 6-4 despite Judge, Wood, and Duran all scoring runs; the .267 OBP is alarming and 46 IP with 46 K is thin rotation usage — needs more from the arms | down
8. North Correa | Oneil Cruz went off for 4 HR and 10 RBI and they still only won 7-3; rotation outside Senga needs reinforcement and the Austin Wells addition at catcher is smart | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 26 K and 1.65 ERA was absolutely elite but Freddie Freeman's .226 OBP is deeply concerning; four waiver adds show urgency and energy from the manager | same

10. Hiroshima Radiation | Dropped 8-1 in a bloodbath despite Carlos Correa scoring 8 runs and Schwarber going deep 3 times; a 43 K staff week is the core problem and Colin Rea won't fix it alone | down
11. Canberra Wombats | Lost 6-4 but Altuve's .577 OBP, Walker, Henderson, and Díaz all went deep — the offense is real; Aaron Nola and Seth Lugo both getting torched is the recurring nightmare | same

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7-3 despite a 3.19 ERA and 4 QS — the pitching did its job and still couldn't cover for the offense; Parker Messick and Gausman both brilliant and it didn't matter | same
13. Benchwarmers | Won 5-4 but this is a smoke-and-mirrors record; Corbin Carroll and Cody Bellinger are real pieces but the rotation logged only 46 IP and the power numbers are near-invisible | same

14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Got blanked 9-0 in every single category — zero wins, zero stolen bases, a .270 OBP, and Imanaga struggled again; the aggressive waiver moves including Masyn Winn and Dustin May signal panic mode | down
waiver_highlights: Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Keaton Winn, Braxton Garrett, Josh Jung, Noelvi Marte; dropped 3 | Aggressive four-player haul shows the manager knows the roster has holes — Jung and Marte add positional versatility, and the two SP adds help a rotation that outside Harrison is threadbare

General Hospital | B+ | Added Kumar Rocker; dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk, Cristian Javier | Clearing three underperformers to land Rocker is exactly the right move for a team whose pitching is already its identity — if Rocker is healthy, this is a steal

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | B | Added Masyn Winn, Dustin May, Ildemaro Vargas, Scott Barlow; dropped 4 | After a 9-0 disaster the manager responded with urgency — Masyn Winn addresses the SB and OBP gaps, Dustin May is a high-upside SP add if healthy, but four moves in one week risks roster instability

North Correa | C+ | Added Austin Wells, Brayan Rocchio; dropped J.T. Realmuto, Brooks Lee | Upgrading at catcher from a struggling Realmuto to Wells makes sense, but Rocchio is a speculative add who doesn't move the needle significantly

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Victor Vodnik; dropped Mark Vientos | Losing Vientos' power upside to add a fringe reliever in a week when the offense finally exploded feels like a missed opportunity to double down on a winning formula
